content: The angle subtended by the vector $$A = 4\widehat i + 3\widehat j + 12\widehat k$$ with the x-axis is
Six vectors $\vec{a}, \vec{b}, \vec{c}, \vec{d}, \vec{e}$ and $\vec{f}$ have the magnitudes and directions indicated in the figure. Which of the following statements is true?
explanation:
$$ \mathbf{b}+\mathbf{e}=\mathbf{R} $$

and clearly, $\mathbf{R} \neq \mathbf{f}$

Similarly option (b), (c) are not correct

$$ \text { Hence, option (d) is correct. } $$

content: Let $$\theta$$ be the angle between two vectors $$\vec{A}$$ and $$\vec{B}$$. If $$\hat{a}_{\perp}$$ is the unit vector perpendicular to $$\vec{A}$$, then the direction of $$ \overrightarrow{\mathrm{B}}-\mathrm{B} \sin \theta \hat{\mathrm{a}}_{\perp} \text { is }$$
explanation:
To determine the direction of the vector expression

$$\overrightarrow{\mathrm{B}} - \mathrm{B} \sin \theta \hat{\mathrm{a}}_{\perp}$$, let's break it down step-by-step:

1. Understanding the given vectors:

Let $$\vec{A}$$ and $$\vec{B}$$ be two vectors with an angle $$\theta$$ between them. The unit vector $$\hat{a}_{\perp}$$ is perpendicular to $$\vec{A}$$.

2. Projection of $$\vec{B}$$:

The term $$\mathrm{B} \sin \theta$$ is equivalent to the magnitude of the component of $$\vec{B}$$ that is perpendicular to $$\vec{A}$$. Thus, multiplying it with $$\hat{a}_{\perp}$$ gives a vector component of $$\vec{B}$$ perpendicular to $$\vec{A}$$, which can be written as:

$$\mathrm{B} \sin \theta \hat{\mathrm{a}}_{\perp}$$

3. Subtracting the perpendicular component from $$\vec{B}$$:

When we subtract this perpendicular component from $$\vec{B}$$, we get:

$$\overrightarrow{\mathrm{B}} - \mathrm{B} \sin \theta \hat{\mathrm{a}}_{\perp}$$

This operation essentially removes the part of $$\vec{B}$$ that is perpendicular to $$\vec{A}$$, leaving only the component of $$\vec{B}$$ that is parallel to $$\vec{A}$$.

4. Conclusion:

The resulting vector will be pointing in the direction along $$\vec{A}$$ because the perpendicular component has been removed. Thus, the direction of

$$\overrightarrow{\mathrm{B}} - \mathrm{B} \sin \theta \hat{\mathrm{a}}_{\perp}$$ is along $$\vec{A}$$.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: along $$\vec{A}$$.

content:
Consider the vectors $$A = \hat i + \hat j - \hat k$$
,$$B = 2\hat i - \hat j + \hat k$$ and $$C = {1 \over {\sqrt 5 }}\left( {\hat i - 2\hat j + 2\hat k} \right)$$
. What is the value of C. (A$$ \times $$ B) ?
explanation:
Given, $$A = \hat i + \hat j - \hat k$$ , $$B = 2\hat i - \hat j + \hat k$$ , $$C = {1 \over {\sqrt 5 }}\left( {\hat i - 2\hat j + 2\hat k} \right)$$

$$ \because $$ $$A \times B = \left| {\matrix{ {\hat i} & {\hat j} & {\hat k} \cr 1 & 1 & { - 1} \cr 2 & { - 1} & 1 \cr } } \right| = - 3\hat j - 3\hat k$$

$$ \therefore $$ $$C.\left( {A \times B} \right) = {1 \over {\sqrt 5 }}\left( {\hat i - 2\hat j + 2\hat k} \right)\left( { - 3\hat j - 3\hat k} \right)$$

= $${6 \over {\sqrt 5 }} - {6 \over {\sqrt 5 }} = 0$$

content: In a triangle ABC, the sides AB and AC are represented by the vectors $$3\widehat i + \widehat j + \widehat k$$ and $$\widehat i + 2\widehat j + \widehat k$$, respectively. Calculate the angle $$\angle ABC$$.
explanation:
$$AB = 3\widehat i + \widehat j + \widehat k$$ and $$AC = \widehat i + 2\widehat j + \widehat k$$

$$ \therefore $$ $$BA = - (3\widehat i + \widehat j + \widehat k)$$

$$\angle ABC$$ is angle between BA and BC


$$BC = AC + BA$$

$$ = AC - AB$$ $$ \because $$ $$(BA = - AB)$$

$$ = \widehat i + 2\widehat j + \widehat k - (3\widehat i + \widehat j + \widehat k) = - 2\widehat i + \widehat j$$

$$ \therefore $$ $$BA\,.\,BC = |BA||BC|\cos \theta - (3\widehat i + \widehat j + \widehat k)\,.\,( - 2\widehat i + \widehat j)$$

$$ = (\sqrt {{3^2} + {1^2} + {1^2}} )\,.\,(\sqrt {{2^2} + {1^2}} )\cos \theta $$

$$ = 6 - 1 = \sqrt {11 \times 5} \,.\,\cos \theta $$

$$ \therefore $$ $$\cos \theta = {5 \over {\sqrt {55} }} = \sqrt {{5 \over {11}}} = \theta = {\cos ^{ - 1}} = \sqrt {{5 \over {11}}} $$

content: Three vectors $$\overrightarrow A $$ = a$$\widehat i$$ + $$\widehat j$$ + $$\widehat k$$; $$\overrightarrow B $$ = $$\widehat i$$ + b$$\widehat j$$ + $$\widehat k$$ and $$\overrightarrow C $$ = $$\widehat i$$ + $$\widehat j$$ + c$$\widehat k$$ are mutually perpendicular ($$\widehat i$$, $$\widehat j$$ and k are unit vectors along X, Y and Z-axes respectively). The respective values of a, b and c are
explanation:
Three vectors are mutually perpendicular so the scalar or dot product of two vectors will zero. So,

$$A.B = 0,B.C = 0,A.C = 0$$

So, $$A.B = (a\widehat i + \widehat j + \widehat k)\,.\,(\widehat i + b\widehat j + \widehat k) = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow a + b + 1 = 0$$ .... (i)

$$B.C = \widehat i + \widehat j + \widehat k\,.\,\widehat i + \widehat j + \widehat k = $$

$$ \Rightarrow 1 + b + c = 0$$ ....(ii)

$$A.C = (a\widehat i + \widehat j + \widehat k)\,.\,(\widehat i + \widehat j + c\widehat k) = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow a + 1 + c = 0$$ .... (iii)

Addition of Eqs. (i), (ii) and (iii),

$$2(a + b + c) + 3 = 0$$

$$a + b + c = - {3 \over 2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow - 1 + c = - {3 \over 2}$$

$$c = - {3 \over 2} + 1$$

$$ = - {1 \over 2} = 0.5$$

$$a = - {1 \over 2}$$

$$b = - {1 \over 2}$$

$$c = - {1 \over 2}$$

content: The vectors $$\overrightarrow A $$ and $$\overrightarrow B $$ are such that | $$\overrightarrow A $$ + $$\overrightarrow B $$ | = | $$\overrightarrow A $$ $$-$$ $$\overrightarrow B $$ |. The angle between the two vectors will be
explanation:
Given, | A + B | = | A $$-$$ B |

$$ \therefore $$ (A + B)2 = (A $$-$$ B)2

A2 + B2 + 2AB cos $$\theta$$ = A2 + B2 $$-$$ 2 AB cos $$\theta$$

2 AB cos $$\theta$$ + 2 AB cos $$\theta$$ = 0

4 AB cos $$\theta$$ = 0

Here, A $$ \ne $$ 0, B $$ \ne $$ 0

$$ \therefore $$ cos $$\theta$$ = 0

or $$\theta$$ = 90$$^\circ$$
